text: Insight — the sudden "aha" that solves a problem you stopped consciously trying to solve — has a neural signature. Using EEG, Mark Jung-Beeman and John Kounios tracked the moment of creative breakthrough to a burst of gamma-band activity in the right anterior temporal lobe, arriving about 300 milliseconds before subjects reported the solution. Crucially, this burst was preceded by a quieting of visual cortex, as if the brain had to stop processing the outside world to hear a signal arising from within. Insight does not reward persistence; it rewards the willingness to look away.
question: According to the passage, what neural event preceded the gamma-band burst associated with insight?

options.text: Increased activity in the left prefrontal cortex
A spike in activity in the hippocampus
A quieting of the visual cortex
